compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## What
Prove the batch write reduces commit count and does not break crash-resume: a before/after
commit-count benchmark on a synthetic many-event import, plus rollback + crash-resume tests.
**Depends on B1 (890d) and B2 (6386)** — the primitive and the importer adoption must exist.

## Why
Batching changes commit granularity (N events per commit). We must demonstrate the win
(commit-count reduction) and prove the safety properties: all-or-nothing per commit, and
that a crash mid-batch plus the `source_id` re-scan resumes cleanly. NOTE: the importer's
existing LINE parsing is fail-open (a malformed NDJSON line is skipped); the whole-chunk
rollback proven here is a property of the BATCH PRIMITIVE (`batch_stage_and_commit`) — one
event that fails validation inside a chunk rolls back that chunk's already-staged events —
not of the importer's line parser.

## Scope
- Benchmark test: build a synthetic N=3000 NDJSON where each ticket has 1 CREATE + 1 comment
  and no parents/file-impact/verify/links/statuses (so only Pass 1 and Pass 2d carry events),
  run `import_tickets`, and assert the commit-count DELTA (`int(subprocess.run(["git","-C",
  tracker,"rev-list","--count","HEAD"],capture_output=True,text=True).stdout)` after − before)
  == `2*ceil(3000/256)` (=24), >=10x fewer than the ~6000 per-event commits.
- Malformed-event-mid-chunk test (batch primitive level): a chunk containing one event that
  fails validation triggers whole-chunk rollback — assert index clean (`git diff --cached
  --name-only` empty) AND commit count unchanged (`git rev-list --count HEAD`), and no
  chunk event files left on disk.
- Crash-resume test: simulate a crash before a chunk's `git commit` (e.g. monkeypatch the
  commit to raise after staging); assert the store is left whole-commit-or-none, then re-run
  the import and assert the `source_id` re-scan skips committed events and re-emits the rest.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Benchmark test asserts the commit-count DELTA (`git rev-list --count HEAD` after − before; baseline `init_repo` commits excluded via the delta) == `2*ceil(3000/256)` (=24) for a synthetic N=3000 CREATE+comment import, >=10x fewer than the ~6000 per-event commits
- [ ] Malformed-event-mid-chunk test: a chunk containing an event dict that fails the primitive's validation (invalid `event_type` / missing `uuid`) rolls the whole chunk back — assert BOTH `git diff --cached --name-only` is empty (index clean, no phantom staged blob) AND `git rev-list --count HEAD` is unchanged from before the failed flush (proving no commit was written), and none of the chunk's event files exist on disk
- [ ] Crash-resume test on a CREATE-only synthetic file (N=1000 tickets, so Pass 1 is the only batched pass and committed-event count == committed-ticket count): monkeypatch `event_append.subprocess.run` so `git commit` succeeds for the first K=2 256-chunks then raises after staging on the 3rd chunk. Assert (i) whole-commit-or-none: `git diff --cached --name-only` empty (B1's rollback ran) and `rebar.list_tickets` count == exactly 512 (no partial chunk); undo the monkeypatch and re-run the same import and assert (ii) `rebar.list_tickets` returns all 1000 tickets exactly once (no duplicates/losses) and (iii) the re-run adds exactly `ceil(488/256)=2` chunks' worth of commits (`git rev-list --count HEAD` delta), proving the source_id re-scan skipped the 512 already-committed tickets
- [ ] Named regression suites green (verified paths): `tests/unit/test_store_batch_write.py`, `tests/interfaces/store/test_reducer_single_source.py` + `tests/unit/test_reducer_status_fork_record.py` (replay), `tests/unit/test_sync_union_recovery.py` + `tests/interfaces/store/test_gitattributes_merge.py` (union-merge convergence), `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_compact_negative.py` + `tests/unit/test_repo_snapshot.py` + `tests/unit/test_snapshot_cache.py` (SNAPSHOT compaction)
- [ ] `make test` green

file_impact.reason: commit-count benchmark, malformed-chunk rollback, crash-resume tests

compiled_state.title: B4: commit-count benchmark + crash-resume/rollback tests for batched writes
